name: Aýna to El Salero returning via the Camino to Puente de la Alcudia — circular
name_original: Aýna hasta El Salero volviendo por el Camino a Puente de la Alcudia — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Ayna offers diverse outdoor activities within a mountainous landscape in Spain. The region features dramatic terrain with rolling hills and deep valleys, carved by the Mundo River which creates impressive canyons. Hikers can explore lush pine and holm oak forests, wild valleys, and unique rock formations. The area is also home to the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima natural park, providing varied environments for exploration. Best hiking trails around Ayna The most popular hiking route is Sendero del Mundo (GR 67) Aýna Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete, offering views of the Mundo River canyon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aýna to El Salero returning via the Camino to Puente de la Alcudia — circular, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path winding through varied local landscapes. Local hikers also love the Mundo Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through the Mundo River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Ayna is defined by mountainous terrain, river canyons, and dense forests of pine and holm oak. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in Ayna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Ayna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ayna?
A: Ayna offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 30 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, including easy walks, moderate ascents, and more challenging paths.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Ayna?
A: Yes, Ayna has several easy hiking trails perfect for families. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1089125163/ayna-to-el-salero-returning-via-the-camino-to-puente-de-la-alcudia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna to El Salero returning via the Camino to Puente de la Alcudia — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.0-mile (6.4 km) path that winds through varied local landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Ayna?
A: Hiking in Ayna immerses you in a dramatic mountainous landscape. You'll traverse rolling hills, deep valleys carved by the Mundo River, and impressive canyons. The trails often lead through lush pine and holm oak forests, revealing wild valleys and unique rock formations. The area is also part of the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima natural park, offering diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Ayna?
A: Yes, the region is home to the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5318691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. This 10-meter waterfall on the Bogarra river is set within an impressive natural environment of pine and holm oak forests, making it a popular spot, especially during warmer months.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Ayna?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Ayna are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068274416/sendero-del-mundo-gr-67-ayna-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Mundo (GR 67) Aýna Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.0-mile (12.8 km) trail offering views of the Mundo River canyon.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore on a hike near Ayna?
A: Ayna offers a blend of natural beauty and history. You can explore the ruins of &lt;strong&gt;Yedra Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, an 11th-century Islamic fortress, which adds a historical dimension to your hike. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;Cueva del Niño&lt;/strong&gt; features local rock art, with visits often organized through the local tourism office.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views during my hike in Ayna?
A: Ayna is renowned for its breathtaking viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador La Rodea Grande&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5812222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Infiernos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning panoramic vistas of the valleys, the Mundo River, and the unique rock formations upon which the town is situated. Access to these often involves paths winding through forests.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ayna?
A: The hiking routes in Ayna are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the stunning river canyons, and the well-preserved natural environment.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Ayna?
A: Yes, Ayna provides challenging options for experienced hikers. While specific routes vary, the region features trails with significant elevation changes and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17266717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna loop from Aýna&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 480 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ayna?
A: Ayna's mountainous climate makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but popular spots like the Batán Waterfall are particularly refreshing. Winter hiking is also possible, though some higher elevation trails might require more preparation.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Ayna generally dog-friendly?
A: Ayna's natural environment, with its extensive forests and open trails, is generally welcoming for dogs. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural park areas or near wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any 'hidden gem' trails or less-known spots to explore in Ayna?
A: Ayna is rich in 'hidden gems' due to its rugged and diverse terrain. Beyond the main trails, exploring the winding paths through pine and holm oak forests can lead to secluded viewpoints and quiet stretches of the Mundo River, offering a sense of discovery away from more frequented areas.
tips: The viewpoint also called "Sidecar" for the replica of the scooter from the film "Amanece que no es poco" by José Luis Cuerda. The film was shot between the towns of Aýna, Molinicos and Letur in 1988. The viewpoint offers wonderful views of the rocks on which the town of Aýna is located. Very recommendable.

A charming town located in a mountainous landscape surrounded by hills and valleys. It has a long history dating back to Roman times, and has been inhabited by different cultures over the centuries. It is also known for its natural beauty, with the Mundo river and the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima natural park located close to the town.
A church of very simple construction and decoration, dedicated to the patron saint of the town "La Virgen de lo Alto". The tourist office organizes visits.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Heights
